---
output:
  xaringan::moon_reader:
    css: ["default", "extra.css"]
    lib_dir: libs
    seal: false
    nature:
      highlightStyle: github
      highlightLines: true
      countIncrementalSlides: false
      ratio: '16:9'
---

```{r, echo = FALSE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE}
##xaringan::inf_mr()
## For offline work: https://bookdown.org/yihui/rmarkdown/some-tips.html#working-offline
## Images not appearing? Put images folder inside the libs folder as that is the main data directory

library(tidyverse)
library(readxl)
library(stargazer)
##library(kableExtra)
##library(modelr)

knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE,
                      eval = TRUE,
                      error = FALSE,
                      message = FALSE,
                      warning = FALSE,
                      comment = NA)
```

class: slideblue

.size80[**Today's Agenda**]

<br>

.size50[.center[
Exploring the Success Stories (I)]

- Command & control regulations addressing environmental problems
]

<br>

.center[.size40[
  Justin Leinaweaver (Spring 2022)
]]

???

## Prep for Class
1. Review submitted cases
    - https://docs.google.com/document/d/1dDKvveHlHhms-TSNpxn4fOjk9tTYnjQx4sqUVJtm81E/edit?usp=sharing





---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-green_blue_swirl_side.jpg')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center

class: middle

# Paper 2
.size45[
Write a report analyzing the pros and cons of our four policy design options for addressing your specific environmental problem. 

Which is the "right" choice for your work this term?

- Support all claims with evidence
]

???

Keep your eye on the next target: Paper 2.

- In that paper you will be asked to evaluate all four of the approaches we study in class from the perspective of your chosen environmental problem.

### Any questions on the prompt?



---

class: middle, slideblue

.size50[**Today: Command & Control Cases**]

.size45[
Find a real world example of .textblue[**this approach**] being used to .textblue[**successfully**] address an environmental problem .textblue[**similar to the one in your project**].
]

.size45[
.textblue[**Present as an argument**]: This case shows that addressing environmental problem X can be done successfully using C&C regulations.
]

???

### Does everybody have an argument to present and discuss today?

<br>

Let's start with some speed dating to warm up!

Pair off (10 mins): Each person take 5 minutes to present the case argument you brought for today.

<br>

New pairs, do it again! (10 mins)

<br>

Last time, new pairs do it again! (10 mins)

<br>

Ok, at this point you've heard three different arguments refined your own case argument three times.

Now let's try to extract lessons from all of them.

Everybody form groups of four and spread out around the room.




---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-forest_v2.png')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: middle

.size60[.center[
Identify the .textblue[**characteristics**] associated with .textblue[**successful**] use of a .textblue[**command and control**] regulatory approach to environmental problem-solving]]

???

Here's the over-arching goal of our group-led evaluation.

I'd like us to compare and contrast across all the cases you heard today to help us deepen our understanding of the C&C approach to environmental problem-solving.

### Make sense?



---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-forest_v2.png')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: middle

.size50[.center[
Identify the .textblue[**characteristics**] associated with .textblue[**successful**] use of a .textblue[**command and control**] regulatory approach to environmental problem-solving]]

.size50[
1) How specifically do these regulations target the problem? 
]

???

Ok, groups, first prompt.

Compare and contrast your cases in order to help us identify how the regulations target the problem.

Any environmental problem is likely to be large and multi-faceted, but I'm willing to bet these regulations are highly targeted.

Explore that as a group and report back to the class.

<br>

Collect responses *ON BOARD* and discuss: 'Problem Targeting'

- ?



---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-forest_v2.png')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: middle

.size50[.center[
Identify the .textblue[**characteristics**] associated with .textblue[**successful**] use of a .textblue[**command and control**] regulatory approach to environmental problem-solving]]

.size50[
2) What kinds of stakeholders do these regulations target? 

- Any carrots included or purely a stick?
]

???

Ok, groups, second prompt.

Compare and contrast your cases in order to help us identify how the regulations target the stakeholders.

- Are the regulations purely punitive or are there also incentives included?

Explore that as a group and report back to the class.

<br>

Collect responses *ON BOARD* and discuss: 'Stakeholder Targeting'

- ?



---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-forest_v2.png')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: middle

.size50[.center[
Identify the .textblue[**characteristics**] associated with .textblue[**successful**] use of a .textblue[**command and control**] regulatory approach to environmental problem-solving]]

.size50[
3) What kinds of local institutions exist to support and/or reinforce these regulations? 
]

???

Ok, groups, final prompt.

Compare and contrast your cases in order to help us identify how the regulations fit into the established local system.

- Are the regulations supported by institutional actors or built into established processes?

Explore that as a group and report back to the class.

<br>

Collect responses *ON BOARD* and discuss: 'Institutional Support'

- ?



---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-forest_v2.png')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: middle, center

.size55[**Best advice / things to remember when designing command and control regulations?**]

???

Bottom line time.

### In a practical sense, what guidance can we offer policy-makers considering using a C&C approach to solving environmental problems?

<br>

- Depends on balance of "dirty" vs "clean" industries in the target economy

- Must be paired with assistance

- Is there any way to do this that isn't "big government" getting bigger? Easy to say shut down coal mining and we'll retrain those workers but in reality how would this work? Train for what? Forced relocation to jobs? Government picking winners and losers?

- Proposed to discuss: C&C most realistic if changes are small OR industries carefully targeted.

- Enforcement at the individual level is hard if community doesn't buy-in



---

background-image: url('libs/Images/11-1-Green_taxation.jpg')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: center

???

Next week we explore our next policy design, green taxes!

Make sure you consolidate and clean up your notes from this week.

You might end up making C&C central to your paper!
